skill.name: linkedin-easy-apply
skill.description: Automate LinkedIn Easy Apply searches with Puppeteer, resume upload, role filtering, daily state, and conservative answer rules.
skill.summary: Guides an agent through LinkedIn Easy Apply automation with resume checks and guarded submissions.
skill.author: ralyodio

security_audit.summary: Static code-execution and weak-crypto alerts are mostly false positives caused by Markdown backticks and keyword matching. The skill still has medium risk because it guides authenticated LinkedIn browser automation, persistent profile use, network access, and sandbox-disabled Chromium execution.

medium_findings.title: Authenticated Browser Automation With Account Actions
medium_findings.description: The skill directs an agent to use a logged-in LinkedIn browser session and submit applications. This is user-authorized automation, but it can act on an account and should require explicit operator control.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions explicitly launch a browser profile and click through application submission steps. No evidence found of credential theft or unauthorized exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: Persistent Browser Profile May Contain Session Data
medium_findings.description: The skill recommends a persistent Chrome profile for manual LinkedIn login. That profile can contain cookies and session data, so storage permissions and cleanup matter.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The profile path is explicit and tied to authenticated browser use. The file does not instruct copying or transmitting the profile.

medium_findings.title: Chromium Sandbox Disabled
medium_findings.description: The Puppeteer launch arguments disable Chromium sandbox protections. This can increase impact if a browser exploit or malicious page is encountered.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The launch arguments directly include sandbox-disabling flags. This is a common container workaround, but it weakens browser isolation.

low_findings.title: Expected LinkedIn Network Access
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL points to LinkedIn job application pages, which matches the stated purpose. No evidence found of third-party exfiltration endpoints.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only observed hardcoded network destination is LinkedIn. This is expected for an Easy Apply automation skill.

low_findings.title: Temporary Logs May Contain Application History
low_findings.description: The skill stores state and result logs under /tmp. These logs can expose job targets and application outcomes on shared systems.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The log and state paths are explicit. The exact logged fields are not implemented in this file, so the data exposure risk is limited.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer Keyword Hits Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The Ruby backtick, weak-crypto, sensitive environment-file, and system-reconnaissance alerts come from Markdown formatting or ordinary text. No evidence found of those behaviors.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are Markdown examples, process environment configuration, or plain prose. They do not define Ruby execution, cryptography, .env access, or system reconnaissance.

dangerous_patterns.title: Sandbox-Disabled Browser Launch Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The Puppeteer configuration launches Chromium with sandbox protections disabled. This pattern should be avoided unless required by the runtime.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The sandbox flags are present in the documented launch arguments. The risk is contextual but technically clear.

dangerous_patterns.title: Persistent Authenticated Profile Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The workflow relies on a reusable browser profile for manual login. This stores authenticated session material outside the skill file.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The safety rules and environment sample confirm persistent profile use. The skill also states it contains no credentials.

dangerous_patterns.title: Automated Submission Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The workflow allows clicking through review and final submission when required fields are known. This can create real-world account actions.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The multi-step loop explicitly includes final submission. It includes guardrails for unknown fields, reducing but not removing operational risk.

content.value_statement: Manual job applications take time and are easy to repeat poorly. This skill guides an agent through filtered LinkedIn Easy Apply workflows with resume checks and guarded submissions.
actual_capabilities: Builds LinkedIn Jobs searches with Easy Apply filters and role keywords.
Uses Puppeteer and Chromium patterns for a logged-in browser session.
Uploads or selects a verified resume PDF during applications.
Skips unknown, subjective, salary, and custom essay questions.
Keeps daily state and JSONL logs to avoid duplicate applications.
Reports submitted and skipped jobs with concise reasons.
limitations: Requires a user-managed LinkedIn login and browser profile.
Does not bypass CAPTCHA, MFA, identity checks, or security prompts.
Cannot safely answer facts not present in the resume or user instructions.
May be affected by LinkedIn UI changes, rate limits, or account policies.

use_cases.title: Run focused daily applications
use_cases.description: Search matching jobs, apply only when required facts are known, and keep state for the next run.

use_cases.title: Screen remote roles before applying
use_cases.description: Check titles and full descriptions for remote constraints before submitting applications.

use_cases.title: Audit application automation rules
use_cases.description: Review conservative answer rules, skip criteria, and logging expectations before running an agent.

prompt_templates.title: Start a dry run
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to run a dry LinkedIn Easy Apply search for software engineer roles. Do not submit applications. Report matches and skip reasons.

prompt_templates.title: Apply with verified facts
prompt_templates.prompt: Use my verified resume PDF and apply to at most five matching Easy Apply roles. Skip any job with unknown required questions.

prompt_templates.title: Target remote AI roles
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for remote AI engineer and LLM engineer roles in the United States. Confirm remote status from the full description before applying.

prompt_templates.title: Maintain daily state
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the daily workflow using the existing state directory. Avoid duplicates, summarize new submissions, and list skipped jobs with exact reasons.

output_examples.input: Run a dry search for Codex and Claude-related engineering roles.
output_examples.output: The agent returns matching job titles, companies, LinkedIn URLs, and reasons each job would be submitted or skipped.

output_examples.input: Apply to up to three verified remote roles using my resume.
output_examples.output: The agent reports submitted applications, already submitted jobs, skipped jobs, and the state and log file locations.

output_examples.input: Review the daily run for unknown questions.
output_examples.output: The agent lists questions it refused to answer, explains missing facts, and recommends user-provided answers for future runs.

best_practices: Start with DRY_RUN enabled and review skipped jobs before allowing submissions.
Use a dedicated browser profile and protect its file permissions.
Provide verified answers for work authorization, location, and experience before running.
anti_patterns: Do not store LinkedIn credentials, cookies, or private profile data in the skill.
Do not answer salary, essay, or subjective questions without explicit user input.
Do not continue through CAPTCHA, MFA, identity checks, or suspicious-login prompts.

faq.question: Does this skill include LinkedIn credentials?
faq.answer: No. It instructs the user to log in manually through a persistent browser profile.

faq.question: Can it submit applications automatically?
faq.answer: Yes, but only when required fields are known and the operator allows submissions.

faq.question: Can it answer custom questions?
faq.answer: It should skip custom essays, salary expectations, and unclear questions unless the user supplies approved answers.

faq.question: Does it work for remote-only searches?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports remote filters and asks the agent to inspect full descriptions for hybrid constraints.

faq.question: What files does it write?
faq.answer: It recommends state and result logs under a temporary LinkedIn Easy Apply directory.

faq.question: What is the main security caution?
faq.answer: The main caution is protecting the authenticated browser profile and supervising real application submissions.
